# Grove

Self-hosted household finance app — import bank statements, categorize spending, track net worth, and get an AI-generated year-in-review. Built for a real household and running in production on Koyeb + Postgres.

**AI integrations:** year-in-review narrative generated by the **Anthropic Claude API**; payslip PDF data extraction via the **OpenAI API**.

---

## Features

- **Bank import** — parse Bank of America PDF e-statements and OFX files (Chase, Citi, Wealthfront). Near-duplicate detection handles overlapping imports from the same account period.
- **Transaction categorization** — rule-based engine with substring/regex pattern matching; bulk category assignment; manual override per transaction.
- **Dashboard** — monthly income vs. spending, top categories by spend, savings rate, recurring payment detection, and a cash-flow summary card.
- **Net Worth** — balance sheet with manual balance snapshots per account, real estate tracking with Redfin valuation refresh, and historical net worth chart.
- **Payslips** — upload payslip PDFs; OpenAI extracts structured pay data (gross, taxes, deductions, YTD totals). Deposit matching links payslip to the corresponding bank credit.
- **Year-in-review** — annual summary: income, spending, investments, net worth change, top merchants, best/worst months — plus a narrative paragraph generated by Claude.
- **Notifications** — in-app notification center with configurable per-type preferences; alerts for budget thresholds, large transactions, import completions, and backup events.
- **Multi-person household** — accounts, transactions, payslips, and net worth snapshots attributed per member; all views filter by individual or household.
- **Self-hosted** — your financial data stays on your own Postgres instance. The only external calls are to the AI APIs listed above (optional, feature-gated by env vars).

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Backend | Node.js 20 · Express · TypeScript · Zod |
| Frontend | React 18 · Vite · Mantine UI · TypeScript |
| Database | Postgres 18 — raw SQL via `postgres.js` |
| AI | Anthropic Claude API · OpenAI API |
| Auth | JWT · bcrypt |
| Testing | Vitest — backend integration tests against real Postgres + frontend unit tests |
| Hosting | Koyeb (managed Node.js + Postgres) |

## Quick Start (local dev)

**Prerequisites:** Node.js 20.x, npm, and a Postgres instance (Docker Compose file included for local port 5433).

```bash
# 1. Start local Postgres
docker compose up -d

# 2. Install dependencies, run migrations, and seed sample data
npm run setup

# 3. Start API (port 4000) + frontend (port 3000)
npm run start:dev

# 4. Stop services
npm run stop:dev
```

`npm run setup` creates `.env` from `.env.example` — edit `JWT_SECRET` and `DATABASE_*` before first run.

Interactive alternative: `npm run dev` (API) and `npm run dev:frontend` in separate terminals.

### Common database commands

| Goal | Command |
|------|---------|
| First-time setup (install + migrate + seed) | `npm run setup` |
| Seed only (no install) | `npm run db:seed` |
| Seed with dev sample accounts | `npm run db:seed:dev` |
| Wipe and reseed | `npm run db:reset:dev` |

---

## Production Deployment

Grove runs in production on **Koyeb** (managed hosting) and has been tested on **OCI Always Free** (self-hosted, $0/month). Both paths are fully documented.

Deployment options:
- **Koyeb** — connect your GitHub repo, set env vars in the dashboard, done. Managed Postgres included.
- **OCI Always Free** — Ampere A1 VM (4 OCPU / 24 GB RAM), self-hosted Postgres, nginx + Let's Encrypt. Full step-by-step in the Admin Guide.
- **Any Linux VM or Docker host** — standard Node.js + Postgres setup.

See [`docs/ADMIN_GUIDE.md`](docs/ADMIN_GUIDE.md) for the complete deployment guide, environment variable reference, and operations runbook.

---

## Quality Checks

```bash
npm test        # backend + frontend test suites
npm run lint    # ESLint across both workspaces
npm run build   # production build (backend + frontend)
```

Backend tests run against a real Postgres instance (not mocked). See [`docs/ADMIN_GUIDE.md`](docs/ADMIN_GUIDE.md) §2 for test database setup.

## Contributing

This is personal finance software built for and running in a real household. The codebase is open under the MIT license — you're welcome to fork it, adapt it, or submit issues and pull requests.

A few things to know before diving in:
- BoA PDF is the primary import format; Chase/Citi use OFX. See the bank adapter system in `backend/src/modules/imports/profiles/`.
- Classification is rule-based only — no LLM categorization. OpenAI is used exclusively for payslip PDF extraction.
- All financial data stays on your own Postgres instance. No telemetry, no analytics, no external data calls beyond the optional AI APIs.

See [`docs/ADMIN_GUIDE.md`](docs/ADMIN_GUIDE.md) to get a local instance running, and [`docs/PRD_AND_CRS.md`](docs/PRD_AND_CRS.md) for architecture decisions and design rationale.
